description: Fix antigravity model routing bug where MITM-mapped model prefixes fall through to commandcode. The MITM handler maps antigravity models to strings like 'kimi/model-name' or 'moonshotai/Kimi-K2.6'. When these reach the SSE chat handler directly, parseModel splits them into provider='moonshotai' + model='Kimi-K2.6'. Since 'moonshotai' is not in KNOWN_PROVIDER_IDS, it incorrectly falls back to commandcode. The fix: expand KNOWN_PROVIDER_IDS to include all provider aliases (from ALIAS_TO_ID), add the OAUTH_PROVIDERS keys explicitly, and also check if the parsed.providerAlias resolves to a known provider via resolveProviderAlias before falling back to commandcode.
steps: Read src/sse/services/model.ts to understand the current KNOWN_PROVIDER_IDS construction and the fallback logic at lines 74-77.
Read open-sse/services/model.ts to understand the ALIAS_TO_PROVIDER_ID map that resolveProviderAlias uses - this already maps ALL provider aliases to their IDs.

Modify src/sse/services/model.ts: Import OAUTH_PROVIDERS and ALIAS_TO_ID (or the full alias set) from providers.ts. Expand KNOWN_PROVIDER_IDS to include Object.keys(OAUTH_PROVIDERS), Object.values(ALIAS_TO_ID). This ensures any valid alias resolves correctly.

Also add a secondary check before the commandcode fallback: if parsed.providerAlias exists and resolveProviderAlias(parsed.providerAlias) resolves to a different ID that IS in KNOWN_PROVIDER_IDS, use that resolved provider instead of falling to commandcode. This handles the case where the model comes in as 'alias/model' where alias maps to a real provider.

Add unit tests in tests/unit/ for the antigravity routing fix: test that 'antigravity/gemini-2.5-flash' resolves to provider='antigravity', test that model strings with aliases like 'ag/gemini-2.5-flash' resolve correctly, test that truly unknown prefixes like 'randomprefix/model' still fall to commandcode.
acceptance_criteria: Model string 'antigravity/gemini-2.5-flash' resolves to provider='antigravity' model='gemini-2.5-flash'
Model string with any known alias prefix (e.g. 'ag/model') resolves to the correct provider
Model string with a truly unknown prefix (not matching any provider ID or alias) still falls back to commandcode
npm run typecheck passes with 0 errors
npm run test passes all tests including new routing tests
